multiplicity_method: Holm across the four registered Exp24 actuator comparisons
criterion: registered task-specific actuator direction is strictly positive; one-sided seed-paired sign-flip p-value must pass Holm familywise correction at alpha=0.05
note: 95% whole-seed bootstrap. Support requires all 30 seeds, the complete 10-cell grid, shared two-dimensional oracle controls, and a training-only joint state/rate/gain/event budget receipt.

multiplicity_method: Holm across the four registered Exp24 actuator comparisons
criterion: registered task-specific actuator direction is strictly positive; one-sided seed-paired sign-flip p-value must pass Holm familywise correction at alpha=0.05
note: 95% whole-seed bootstrap. Support requires all 30 seeds, the complete 10-cell grid, shared two-dimensional oracle controls, and a training-only joint state/rate/gain/event budget receipt.

multiplicity_method: intersection-union AND over four Holm-corrected components
criterion: all four Holm-corrected direction claims must support (intersection-union AND)
note: This is an oracle actuator-isolation benchmark; it does not establish that a local rule learned the controller. The joint alternative is an intersection over the corrected components.

multiplicity_method: Holm across the four registered Exp24 actuator comparisons
criterion: registered task-specific actuator direction is strictly positive; one-sided seed-paired sign-flip p-value must pass Holm familywise correction at alpha=0.05
note: 95% whole-seed bootstrap. Support requires all 30 seeds, the complete 10-cell grid, shared two-dimensional oracle controls, and a training-only joint state/rate/gain/event budget receipt.

multiplicity_method: Holm across the four registered Exp24 actuator comparisons
criterion: registered task-specific actuator direction is strictly positive; one-sided seed-paired sign-flip p-value must pass Holm familywise correction at alpha=0.05
note: 95% whole-seed bootstrap. Support requires all 30 seeds, the complete 10-cell grid, shared two-dimensional oracle controls, and a training-only joint state/rate/gain/event budget receipt.

comparison: all Exp25 success criteria including cross-session transfer
criterion: every Exp25 component must support (AND)
note: An invalid cross-session protocol can never support the joint claim; it leaves the result inconclusive unless another component conclusively opposes the hypothesis.

comparison: fully-gated - common held-out mean log likelihood in cross-session transfer
criterion: strictly greater; >= 2 animals and >= 2 sessions; every relevant outer fold complete
note: Likelihood effects are normalized per held-out observation, averaged within session, then within animal before whole-animal bootstrap. Neuron and time bin are never independent repeats.

comparison: fully-gated - common held-out mean log likelihood
criterion: strictly greater; >= 2 animals and >= 2 sessions; every relevant outer fold complete
note: Likelihood effects are normalized per held-out observation, averaged within session, then within animal before whole-animal bootstrap. Neuron and time bin are never independent repeats.

criterion: strictly greater; >= 2 animals and >= 2 sessions; every relevant outer fold complete
note: Likelihood effects are normalized per held-out observation, averaged within session, then within animal before whole-animal bootstrap. Neuron and time bin are never independent repeats. Prerequisite `separate-task > common held-out gain` estimate/CI-low=None/None; it must be strictly positive.

criterion: strictly less; >= 2 animals and >= 2 sessions; every relevant outer fold complete
note: Likelihood effects are normalized per held-out observation, averaged within session, then within animal before whole-animal bootstrap. Neuron and time bin are never independent repeats.

comparison: input-gated - state-gated held-out mean log likelihood
criterion: strictly greater; >= 2 animals and >= 2 sessions; every relevant outer fold complete
note: Likelihood effects are normalized per held-out observation, averaged within session, then within animal before whole-animal bootstrap. Neuron and time bin are never independent repeats.

comparison: fully-gated - separate-task held-out mean log likelihood on unseen composition
criterion: strictly greater; >= 2 animals and >= 2 sessions; every relevant outer fold complete
note: Likelihood effects are normalized per held-out observation, averaged within session, then within animal before whole-animal bootstrap. Neuron and time bin are never independent repeats.

multiplicity_method: Holm across four registered Exp31 claims
criterion: lower 95% CI exceeds 0.02 and Holm p < 0.05
note: Primary utility includes forced exploration; selector has no true context, descriptors, unexecuted reward, or candidate utility vector.

comparison: (memory-routing at low reliability) - (memory-routing at high reliability)
multiplicity_method: Holm across four registered Exp31 claims
criterion: lower 95% CI exceeds 0.10 and Holm p < 0.05
note: Reliability is recorded only for evaluation and the oracle upper bound; it is absent from the local selector interface.

multiplicity_method: Holm across four registered Exp31 claims
criterion: lower 95% CI exceeds 0.05 and Holm p < 0.05
note: The control preserves every write, final memory state, cumulative L1/L2, and output magnitude.

multiplicity_method: Holm across four registered Exp31 claims
criterion: lower 95% CI exceeds zero and Holm p < 0.05
note: Mean ratio retained is 0.473; oracle uses a train-only cell map plus true hidden state and is only an upper bound.

criterion: all four component claims support
note: Narrow synthetic controller-identifiability result only; no high-rank E/I carrier or real-data claim.

multiplicity_method: Holm across the three registered Exp32 main-controller contrasts
criterion: lower 95% seed-bootstrap CI exceeds 0.02 and Holm p < 0.05
note: Continuous HMM stream; no reset; includes exploration and switching; 28/30 seed effects are positive.

multiplicity_method: Holm across the three registered Exp32 main-controller contrasts
criterion: lower 95% seed-bootstrap CI exceeds 0.02 and Holm p < 0.05
note: Eligibility-location intervention, not a random shuffle or matched-budget claim; opposite/executed reward-update L1 ratio is 1.215.

comparison: within-seed response to log2 expected feedback per dwell
multiplicity_method: Holm across the three registered Exp32 main-controller contrasts
criterion: lower 95% seed-bootstrap CI exceeds 0.005 and Holm p < 0.05
note: Frozen five-term response model fitted separately within each seed; seed is the inference unit.

comparison: all three Exp32 main-controller criteria plus positive-seed and oracle-retention gates
criterion: all three Holm-corrected components and registered auxiliary gates support
note: Bounded synthetic claim only: two fixed actuators, scalar executed reward, two action values and one control coordinate; no high-rank E/I or real-data claim.

comparison: mean slow-hazard minus fast-hazard local gain at lambda 6.25 and 12.5
criterion: lower 95% seed-bootstrap CI exceeds 0.02 and one-sided paired sign-flip p < 0.05
note: The data do not establish that feedback per dwell alone is insufficient; the separate structural claim remains unresolved.

comparison: main controller claim AND iso-lambda timescale-structure claim
multiplicity_method: intersection-union AND over the two preregistered claim layers
criterion: both registered claim layers must support
note: Main controller layer supports, but the timescale-structure layer is inconclusive; no OR promotion is allowed.
